channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel Entrenches Control Over South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israel warned residents to stay out of a border territory belt and not approach the Litani River area, deepening its grip on southern Lebanon despite the Hezbollah ceasefire. The move signals Israel's intent to maintain a security buffer zone indefinitely.

item.title: 🇮🇷🛢️ Gulf States Fear US-Iran Talks May Cement Tehran's Grip on Hormuz
item.description: Gulf Arab states worry that US-Iran negotiations may only achieve reopening the Strait of Hormuz while leaving Iran with enhanced leverage over the critical waterway. Former Russian President Medvedev's warning crystallized regional fears about Tehran's "golden" control.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇺🇸🇨🇳 Philippines and US Launch Military Exercises Near Taiwan
item.description: Philippine and US forces began annual military exercises featuring maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan. Manila's military chief said the drills would test readiness under "real-world conditions" amid regional tensions with China.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇷🇺 Germany Summons Russian Ambassador Over Direct Threats
item.description: Berlin summoned the Russian ambassador to condemn what it called "direct threats" against targets in Germany, escalating diplomatic tensions between the two countries. The move reflects growing friction between Russia and European nations.

item.title: 🇵🇸🕊️ Board of Peace Envoy Sees Progress on Gaza Disarmament Plan
item.description: The Board of Peace's lead envoy for Gaza said he was "fairly optimistic" that a plan for disarmament of Hamas and other militant groups in Gaza can be agreed, but cautioned it will take time. The comments come as over 60 nations met in Brussels to discuss Gaza stability.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇸🇦 China's Xi Jinping Calls for Hormuz to Remain Open in Phone Call With Saudi Crown Prince
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for normal passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz to be maintained in a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman. Xi urged an immediate ceasefire between the US and Iran and stressed that the waterway's reopening was in the 'common interests of all countries.'

item.title: 🇮🇷🇵🇰 Iran May Join Peace Talks After Pakistan Works to End US Blockade, Official Says
item.description: Iran is considering attending peace talks with the United States in Pakistan after Islamabad moved to end the US naval blockade of Iran's ports, a senior Iranian official told Reuters. The development could revive negotiations ahead of the ceasefire deadline on Wednesday.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 Trump Says Israel Never Talked Him Into War With Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said Israel did not persuade him to attack Iran, rejecting reports that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu influenced his decision. The statement comes amid criticism from right-wing commentators questioning the war's origins.

item.title: ⛪ Israel Condemns Soldier's Desecration of Crucifix in Southern Lebanon
item.description: A photo showing the desecration of a crucifix smashed by an Israeli soldier in a southern Lebanese Christian village drew widespread condemnation from Israeli leaders, the United States and church leaders. The incident has damaged Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

item.title: 🌍 Battle-Scarred Developing Nations Seek Path Out of 'Permacrisis' at IMF Meetings
item.description: Developing country policymakers left the IMF-World Bank meetings frustrated that successive external shocks are derailing efforts to tackle high debt, reform economies and deliver better lives. The Iran war's energy disruptions have hit the world's poorest nations hardest.

item.title: 🚁 US Air Force Seeks Better Infrared Jammers for Combat Rescue Helicopters After Iran Mission
item.description: The US Air Force is seeking better infrared countermeasures for its HH-60W combat rescue helicopters after one took multiple hits from Iranian small-arms fire during a high-profile pilot rescue mission. The aircraft currently lacks a capable advanced infrared countermeasures system, significantly increasing the risk of infrared-guided missile engagement.

item.title: 🇳🇿 New Zealand Declares State of Emergency as Record Rainfall Triggers Wellington Flash Floods
item.description: New Zealand declared a state of emergency for Wellington after the city received 77mm of rain in less than an hour—its heaviest rainfall on record—triggering flash floods, landslides and evacuations. A search is underway for a man at a flooded property, with the Fire Service responding to 150 calls for help overnight.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Europol Hackathon Identifies 45 More Ukrainian Children Forcibly Deported to Russia
item.description: An international open-source investigation coordinated by Europol uncovered information on 45 children forcibly transferred or deported by Russia from occupied areas of Ukraine. The two-day operation produced leads on transportation routes, individuals involved, and facilities where children were taken.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 China Threatens Retaliation Against EU Firms Over Proposed Cybersecurity Rules Targeting Huawei
item.description: China warned it will hit EU firms with reciprocal measures if the bloc targets Chinese companies like Huawei and ZTE under proposed cybersecurity regulations. Beijing's commerce ministry submitted a 30-page document warning of broad retaliation if Chinese firms are designated as 'high risk suppliers' and excluded from EU markets.

item.title: 🇳🇴 Norwegian F-35s Intercept Russian Il-38 Surveillance Aircraft Over Arctic
item.description: Norway confirmed two F-35 fighters were scrambled from Evenes Air Station to intercept a Russian Il-38 maritime patrol aircraft approaching its airspace on April 13. The incident highlights continued Russian military activity near NATO's northern flank amid heightened tensions with the alliance.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Powerful 7.7 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Northern Japan, Triggers Mega-Quake Warning
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake struck off northern Japan's Sanriku coast, triggering tsunami alerts of up to 3 meters and a rare advisory warning of a 1% chance of a mega-quake in the coming week. Over 128,000 residents received evacuation advisories, with bullet trains suspended, though no major damage or nuclear abnormalities were reported.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Over 60 Nations Gather in Brussels for EU-Hosted Gaza Peace Conference
item.description: Representatives from over 60 nations convened in Brussels for talks with Palestinian Prime Minister Mohamed Mustafa on stability and peace in Gaza and the West Bank. The EU aims to increase its relevance in Middle East diplomacy amid the Iran war, with renewed momentum following Hungary's election of a leader more critical of Netanyahu.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraq's Ruling Shia Bloc Races to Choose Prime Minister as US and Iran Watch Closely
item.description: Iraq's Coordination Framework must nominate a prime minister by April 26 as the country balances ties between the US and Iran amid the ongoing conflict. IRGC Quds Force chief Ismail Qaani visited Baghdad to influence the selection, with incumbent PM al-Sudani facing a challenge from pro-Iran former PM Nouri al-Maliki.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Expresses Concern Over US Seizure of Iranian Tanker Touska
item.description: China said it was concerned about the seizure of the Iranian-flagged tanker Touska by US forces, noting the vessel has frequently traveled between China and Iran. The incident adds another layer of complexity to the US-Iran standoff as Beijing navigates its energy and diplomatic interests in the region.

item.title: 🌍 Ugandan and Congolese Troops Rescue 200 Captives From IS-Affiliated Militants
item.description: Ugandan and Congolese soldiers rescued at least 200 civilians in a raid on an Islamist militant camp in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The operation targeted IS-affiliated militants who have been conducting attacks in the volatile region bordering Uganda.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇰🇷 India and South Korea Aim to Double Trade to $50 Billion by 2030 Amid Global Uncertainty
item.description: India and South Korea agreed to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030 during South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's state visit to New Delhi. Both nations seek closer ties amid unpredictable relations with the US under President Trump, signing MoUs on shipbuilding, chips, and technology cooperation.

item.title: 🇸🇮 Slovenia's Prime Minister Golob Fails to Form Government, Opening Door to Right-Wing Coalition
item.description: Slovenia's caretaker Prime Minister Robert Golob admitted his party cannot form a coalition government after razor-thin election results. The failure opens the door for former three-time Premier Janez Janša and his right-wing SDS party to attempt forming a government.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French Prosecutors Summon Elon Musk in Criminal Probe of X Platform Over Abuse Images and Deepfakes
item.description: Elon Musk was summoned by French prosecutors for questioning as part of a widening criminal investigation into X and its AI chatbot Grok. The probe focuses on allegations of child sexual abuse material, deepfakes, and data extraction on the platform, further straining US-EU relations over Big Tech regulation.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu Condemns IDF Soldier Who Smashed Jesus Statue in Lebanon as Backlash Grows
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned 'in the strongest terms' an IDF reservist who smashed a statue of Jesus in a southern Lebanese village, saying he was 'stunned and saddened.' The IDF confirmed the viral photo's authenticity and launched an investigation amid backlash from US conservatives and Christian communities.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇿 Israel's Mossad and Security Agencies Dismantle Iranian Terror Network in Azerbaijan
item.description: Mossad, Shin Bet, and the IDF announced they dismantled a global Iranian terror network operating in Azerbaijan that was targeting Western sites. Top Iranian leaders of the network from Unit 4000 of the IRGC Intelligence were killed, dealing a blow to Tehran's overseas operations.
